title: Ophthalmologic testing systems and methods
abstract: An optical headset comprises an outer frame that is wearable on the face of a user and an optics module that is removably mounted in the outer frame. The optics module can include a display visible to a wearer of the headset and a display visible to others while the headset is being worn. The outside display can be integral to the outer frame and driven via an electrical interface between the headset frame and the removable optics module. The removable optics module can include separate replaceable subcomponents to allow for easy customization. Various components can be included in the optics module including a rotatable fundus camera.
claims: 1. A wearable optical headset system comprising: a generally tubular outer frame extending between a front portion and a back portion along a central axis, the outer frame having an interior and an exterior, the front portion of the outer frame configured to be pressed against a user's face and surround the eyes of the user; a headstrap attached to the outer frame and configured to hold the back of the outer frame against the user's face when the headset is worn; an optics module in an optics module housing having a front surface, a back surface, and a side surface, the optics module comprising (i) computer circuitry including a computer processor, a digital memory connected to the processor and configured to store computer software executable by the processor, and (ii) an optical component comprising at least one of an image display system and an image capture system, the optical component electrically connected to the processor and controllable by the processor in accordance with stored computer software; the optics module housing slidable within the interior of the outer frame along the central axis and removably engaged to the outer frame and where the front of the optics module is visible through the front portion of the outer frame.
